resources.string: %1$s requires an update to the Android Wear app.
%1$s won\'t run unless you update the Android Wear app.
%1$s requires one or more Google Play services that are not currently available. Please contact the developer for assistance.
%1$s won\'t work unless you enable Google Play services.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are missing from your phone.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are missing from your tablet.
The specified account does not exist on this device. Please choose a different account.
An application requires Google Play services to be enabled.
A data connection is required to connect to Google Play services.
An application requires an update for Google Play services.
Error signing in the specified account. Please choose a different account.
Unknown issue with Google Play services.
%1$s relies on Google Play services, which is not supported by your device. Contact the manufacturer for assistance.
%1$s won\'t run unless you update Google Play services.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are currently updating.

Move %1$s in different directions with the arrows on the left. Discover the combined movements by pressing the buttons on the right.\nYou can also try the fun animations and change the mouth gestures.\n The more you play, the more it can do!
You need to wait %1$s minutes before you try again.

The functions of %1$s will change on reprogramming, so it will perform different actions when you press the buttons!\nBut don\'t worry, when you quit the project, you\'ll be able to restore the original functions.
Your score is too low to enter the ranking.\nWhy not have another go?

%1$s will make a sequence of movements. Watch and remember them carefully! Then you have to repeat them by pressing the button with the picture that corresponds to each movement.\n\nIf you repeat the sequence correctly, %1$s will add another move. The more you get it right, the more points you will win to enter the ranking.
You’re a total expert in putting Zowi in action!
It seems that Zowi\'s a bit confused...
With this move, Zowi can do a funny sidestep.
What new thing is Zowi doing?
Zowi has learn a new way to move forward, the trot!
What awesome memory! Zowi loves it.
See how Zowi gets the nervous with the trembles.
You now have the magic animation.
With this editor, you can design your own mouth for Zowi.
Zowi shakes a foot in the air.
Wow! Zowi can sleep standing up without falling over.
Another movement to add to the collection that will make Zowi a star performer!
Now you know Bitbloq! Celebrate with a new dance move in \"3, 2, 1... Action!\".
Zowi also does the Mexican wave.
Discover the \"Zowi, the alarm robot” project.
Create a sequence of 15 movements in “3, 2, 1... Action!”.
Open the app again to get it.
Use the app on 4 different days.
Get eight mouths right in \"Draw the mouth\"
Use the \"GamePad\" and play \"3, 2, 1... Action!\" once.
Discover the \"Bitbloq I: Hello World” project.
To finish up, place Zowi upright and carry out the test movement to confirm that it’s calibrated correctly.\n
When Zowi does a handstand, check that the feet are at the correct angle.
Use this angle as a reference for the left foot.
Use this angle as a reference for the right foot.
Put Zowi in a handstand and check that the legs and head form a straight line.
Use this side as a reference for the left leg.
Use this side as a reference for the right leg.

You are about to adjust the position of Zowi’s legs and feet. It’s very important that you complete this process in full , otherwise Zowi might stop working correctly. Are you sure you want to do this now?\n
Something’s up... Has an error occurred during reprogramming?\nDo you want to restore %1$s’s original program? This will make the buttons on the back will go back to working as they did initially.
With this score you haven\'t entered the ranking yet…
With this score you have entered the ranking.

BQ is an educational technology company. Helping younger children to understand technology, encouraging them to use it and motivating them to develop it is the fuel that powers us. We want to teach them all that we've learnt from designing complex electronic products such as mobile telephones, e-readers and tablets.

Technology rapidly transforms everything it touches. It occupies an increasingly important space in our day-to-day and it determines the way we interact with the people around us.
We can't accurately predict what the world will be like (or what we'll be like) in 20 years' time, but we do know that science and technology will play leading roles in its change.

In this respect, it's essential our children become familiar with its fundamentals from early on.

It is in this context that we created Zowi. 
Zowi is a 21st-century toy, designed with the intention of opening the doors of technology to children.

Through play, Zowi guides your children through the discovery of electronics (hardware), programming (software) and design. It teaches them that technology can be transparent, fun and accessible.

In addition to this, Zowi is an open toy: all its software and assembly plans are Open Source. As a result, your child can delve into the robot's functionality without hindrance. 
The freer knowledge is, the faster we progress.

We hope that your experience with Zowi is as fun and exciting as it was for us giving it life.
\"Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world\".\nNelson Mandela
If you continue, %1$s could switch off during update. The best thing to do is to charge your robot (you can do this at the same time, simply connect it to a PC).
If you continue, %1$s could switch off during the reprogramming process. The best thing to do is to charge your robot (you can do this at the same time, simply connect it to a PC).
If you continue, %1$s could switch off during the reprogramming process. The best thing to do is to charge your robot (you can do this at the same time, simply connect it to a PC).
If you continue, %1$s could switch off during the restoring process. The best thing to do is to charge your robot (you can do this at the same time, simply connect it to a PC).
With this editor, you can design your own mouth for Zowi. \nJust use your finger to draw one on your device.

Zowi’s mouth will change. You need to draw Zowi’s mouth on your device, in the least time possible. \nEach time you get it right, you’ll go up one difficulty level. \nThe more you get right, the more points you´ll win in order to the enter the ranking.

Zowi has one purpose: enabling you to discover technology while having fun. And it wants you to teach it new things! Get to know this friendly robot in its app, where you´ll find fun games and activities. New functions will be unlocked as you play. Discover them all!


 
 Zowi mini-games: - GamePad : Convert your device into a remote and take control of Zowi´s every move. Make it walk, dance, balance on tiptoes, make different mouth gestures and much, much more. - 3, 2, 1... Action! : Program Zowi to perform different actions in the order and at the speed that you want. Press Play and watch it perform them all one by one. - Repeat with Zowi : Can you remember all the moves that Zowi makes? Put your memory to the test! The more movements you get right, the more points you´ll win in order to the enter the ranking! And don´t forget to update the app to make sure you get the latest games! Play and discover: Your robot also learns new things the more you play with it. We set you enjoyable challenges to get you interacting with Zowi and learning more about how it works. With every challenge overcome, new actions and mini-games will appear! Unlock animations, dance steps or fun movements. Set yourself science, music or dance challenges that will teach you more about robotics and the world that surrounds us at the same time. Get to know Bitbloq! The Zowi App will also bring you closer to the world of Bitbloq, a simple and intuitive programming tool which you can use to teach Zowi new skills. Got any queries about Zowi? Visit zowi.bq.com. On the website, you’ll find all the latest news and updates, projects, tutorials and downloadables you need to get the best out of your robot. And don´t forget: the app is designed to interact with Zowi. You can get it at https://store.bq.com/es/robot-juguete-zowi or via our usual distributors. More information at http://www.bq.com/uk/zowi​
Discover Zowi and play with it in your tablet and smartphone.

Zowi is now multilingual! The Zowi App is now available in Spanish, English and German.

What´s more, if you have any issues with your Zowi, you can now easily access the Robot Hospital via the Settings icon to resolve your queries. 

More improvements have also been added so you can enjoy your Zowi to the max.
Do you believe it\'s possible to guess what will happen? \n Reprogram Zowi and ask questions to find out what random means in this project.
How does Zowi decide what to answer?
It knows the answers because it\'s a fortune-telling robot.
It\'s programmed to change answer every three taps.
Is it possible for Zowi to answer “Yes” every time in mode 1?
It\'s highly unlikely, but there\'s a slight chance it could happen.
If the battery is low, it will always happen.

You will have noticed that Zowi can detect when there are objects ahead. But do you know if it can see them at night? Do you know how Zowi\'s \"eyes\" work? Turn off the lights and see if it crashes into the objects or steers clear of them as it does when there\'s light.\n
...are like human eyes. If there´s no light, they can\'t see anything.
...are like cat\'s eyes, they can even see at night.
...don\'t see the light. They actually emit ultrasound.
Emit sounds that hit objects and bounce back.
Are like the eyes of superheroes, they work with X-rays.
How about dismantling Zowi and seeing what\'s inside? It\'s the first step in exploring the world of robotics.\n Use the guide to dismantling Zowi included in the box \n
On opening Zowi\'s head, you\n DIDN’T find:\n
To dismantle Zowi, you will need:
We\'ve compared Zowi\'s parts with a human body. What is Zowi missing?
Robots can use many types of components to move. Do you know which one Zowi has?
The best way to move along flat terrain is with:
Create your first intelligent programs! Learn how to program Zowi to perform different actions, based on the information received through its sensors.
On programming a conditional “If... Do...” block:
It always executes the instruction inside it.
It only executes an instruction inside if a condition is met.
It never executes the instruction inside it.
...is a digital component for measuring distance.
...is an analogue component for measuring the colour of the clouds.
...is an analogue component for measuring distance.
A choreography is a sequence of dance moves. Choose a song you like and invent your own choreography for Zowi to dance in “3, 2, 1... Action!”\n
In “3, 2, 1... Action!” the movements have various options:
5 speeds and the number of repetitions.
3 speeds, the number of repetitions and some of them, the direction.
The choreographies that you create with “3, 2, 1... Action!”:
Can only have up to 10 movements maximum.
Can combine movements with mouth gestures.
Change if you place your hand in front of Zowi.

Isn\'t Zowi one good lookin’ robot? But it\'s not just a pretty face... it\'s got brains to boot! Inside the head, our engineers have had to carefully squeeze in all the parts to make it work. Can you imagine which ones?\n\n And you can personalise it too! Play around with the stickers included in the box to change how it looks.
Zowi\'s head has a certain size and shape...
...so that the circuits and motors fit inside.
A two-legged robot can easily fall over.\n Find out how to keep Zowi upright by learning about the “centre of gravity”.
...to make it easy to keep its balance.
Is inside the motors of the feet.
Is more or less between the eyes.
So you think you’re an expert in Zowi and the app? Want to program your own games and improve Zowi? Take your first steps as a robot expert by programming Zowi with Bitbloq \n
After programming Zowi with Bitbloq and turning Zowi on and off...
...Zowi does what you have programmed it to do.
...Zowi remembers the initial functions (mode 1, mode 2 and mode 3).
...Zowi forgets the program until you have loaded a new one.
The Zowi programs are written in:

Two-legged robots have a wider range of movements than robots on wheels, but they are harder to control. Can you keep Zowi tightly under control? \n 1. Have a look around the house for 4 objects of the same size as Zowi, or something smaller\n 2. Place them near to Zowi and move it using the GamePad, so that it pushes them all together, one by one.
The movements of Zowi allow it to:
If I increase Zowi\'s walking speed, will it always go forward faster?
No. It moves faster but sometimes it won\'t go forward any faster because it slips.
No. If it goes faster it could break down and not go forward.
Yes. Zowi always goes forward faster if the speed of movement is higher.

Following in the footsteps of great artists, we\'re going to learn how to program Zowi to draw. \n\nStick a pencil onto Zowi and program it in “3, 2, 1... Action!” to make different patterns, following the instructions.
Moving the pencil into the position indicated in the project, when Zowi walks forward, it draws:
Moving the pen into the position indicated in the project, when Zowi performs the dance move, it draws:
We give it movement commands: the result will depend on the commands given.
We tell it what we want to draw and it always draws exactly the same thing.
It draws zeros and ones all the time.
The things that Zowi knows how to do are saved in its memory. In this project, we will teach it new skills: we will convert Zowi into an anti-theft alarm and a warden robot!
On reprogramming Zowi with the Alarm program:
The and A and B buttons do something else.
....converts Zowi into a warden that alerts you when nearby movement is detected.
You have answered all the questions correctly.
Error on loading the project program.
We\'re changing the function of the buttons. This means that when you press them, %1$s will respond differently.\n\nDon\'t switch off %1$s or your device until this process is finished.

We have finished changing the buttons. Have a go at pressing them to see what new things it will do.\n\nRemember that when you leave the project, you will be able to restore the initial functions so that the buttons will work as before again.

Has the battery ran out? Maybe an error occurred and it switched itself off? Has it wandered off and not come back yet? No worries, all you need to do is restore the original program again.\nThis time, there’s no way it will escape!

%1$s didn’t pay attention as the original program was being restored and can’t remember everything it knew at the beginning. The good news is, there’s a quick fix. All you need to do is restore the original program again.\nIt will defo pay more attention this time!
%1$s has gone back to working as it did initially. Try pressing the buttons on the back and you’ll see how it remembers everything it knew at the beginning.
%1$s is being restored to the original program... Shhh, here’s a sneaky tip: don’t switch off your device or your %1$s during this process and everything will run like clockwork.

Has the battery ran out? Maybe an error occurred and it switched itself off? Has it wandered off and not come back yet? No worries, all you need to do is reprogram it again.\nThis time, there’s no way it will escape!

%1$s didn’t pay attention during reprogramming and hasn’t learnt all the new tricks. The good news is, there’s a quick fix. All you need to do is reprogram it again.\nIt will pay more attention this time!
%1$s has learnt a load of new tricks. Now you can continue the project, you’ll be amazed!
%1$s is being reprogrammed... Shhh, here’s a sneaky tip: don’t switch off your device or your %1$s during reprogramming and everything will run like clockwork.
Zowi\'s functions have now been modified. Press \"Restore\" to return to the original functions and calibrate it.
Adjust the position of Zowi\'s legs correctly.
Don’t like your robot\'s name? Give it another one!
Are you bored of the name? Have you thought of something more original? You can change it as many times as you want until you find the one you like best.
Your robot has a new name!
Want to start over? Delete your progress and start again from scratch.
Are you sure you want to delete everything you have achieved while playing with Zowi? \n\nIf you do, you won\'t be able to restore them and you\'ll have to start over again... though it could be fun!
The app will no longer detect Zowi.
If you decide to forget Zowi, you will need to connect it to the app again just like you did the first time.
We were not able to forget Zowi.
If you have any problems with your Zowi, you can find the solution in the Hospital.
Zowi´s functions have now been changed. Press \"Restore\" so that the buttons on the back work as they did initially. You can change these functions as many times as you like.
Error on restoring the original functions.
We’re restoring the original functions. This could take a few seconds...
Check if a new version of the app is available on Google Play.
Add gestures, animations and movements here.
The line of actions is empty!
Select gestures, animations and movements and add them to the line of actions. Once you have a combination that you like, press Play to put %1$s into action!

Has the battery ran out? Maybe an error occurred and it switched itself off? Has it wandered off and not come back yet? No worries, all you need to do is update it again.\nThis time, there’s no way it will escape!
%1$s didn’t pay attention during update and has stayed the same. The good news is, there’s a quick fix. All you need to do is update it again.\nIt will defo pay more attention this time!
%1$s is being updated... Shhh, here’s a sneaky tip: don’t switch off your device or your %1$s during update and everything will run like clockwork.
To control Zowi, enter the code 1234.
Oops! \nSomething’s not right… \nRemember Zowi\'s name can only contain letters and numbers.
To control Zowi with the app, switch it on first.
OK, I HAVEN’T GOT A ZOWI
The app is searching for your Zowi, this could take a few seconds...
Bluetooth is not available. Check if your mobile device supports Bluetooth connections.
Bluetooth was not enabled. You need to enable it to connect with a device.
You are not connected to a device
Allow Ad to create a calendar event?
Allow Ad to store image in Picture gallery?
